metadata.description: Scenario templates for generating expert SFT training data

scenarios.name: Outnumbered combat
scenarios.description: Player has fewer combat units than enemy. Must use focus fire + kiting to win.
prompt_variants: We're outnumbered. Handle combat.
They have more units than us. What should we do?
Enemy army is bigger. Manage the fight.
Outnumbered, keep my army alive and fight smart.
Handle this combat — we're at a numbers disadvantage.

scenarios.name: Equal strength combat
scenarios.description: Even armies. Focus fire is the difference-maker.
prompt_variants: Armies are about even. Win the fight.
Equal forces — make every attack count.
We're matched up. Handle the engagement.
Even battle. Micromanage combat to get the edge.

scenarios.name: Army advantage combat
scenarios.description: Player has significant army advantage. Should push aggressively.
prompt_variants: We have a big army advantage. Push and destroy them.
We outnumber them. Time to push.
Crush them — we have the numbers.
Big army lead. Attack their base.

scenarios.name: Tactical retreat scenario
scenarios.description: Army badly damaged, need to retreat and preserve remaining forces.
prompt_variants: We're getting destroyed. Pull back everyone.
Retreat! Save whatever we can.
Army's shattered, fall back to base.
We're losing badly. Get surviving units to safety.

scenarios.name: Late game all-in
scenarios.description: Past tick 4000, must commit to all-in push regardless of army state.
prompt_variants: It's late game. Go all in.
Time's running out. Push everything.
End it now — full commit.
Late game, force a decisive fight.

scenarios.name: Mixed composition micro
scenarios.description: Army has tanks and ranged. Formation + focus fire.
prompt_variants: I have tanks and ranged. Set up formation and fight.
Mixed army — position tanks forward, ranged behind.
Use my Chonks as a frontline for the Hissers.
Arrange my army properly and engage.

scenarios.name: Early game worker assignment
scenarios.description: Start of game, need to send workers to gather food.
prompt_variants: Send idle workers to gather food.
Get my Pawdlers working on the nearest food.
Start gathering — assign workers to resources.
Economy setup: workers to fish ponds.

scenarios.name: Economic expansion
scenarios.description: Need to build more economy buildings and expand resource gathering.
prompt_variants: Build a FishMarket near the food deposits.
Expand our economy — more gathering buildings.
We need more income. Build economy infrastructure.
Set up a second gathering location.

scenarios.name: Worker safety under threat
scenarios.description: Enemy raiders spotted near workers. Need to pull workers to safety.
prompt_variants: Enemy units near our workers! Pull them back.
Protect the Pawdlers — enemies incoming.
Get workers to safety, there's a raid.
Worker defense: enemies spotted near gathering area.

scenarios.name: GPU core gathering priority
scenarios.description: Need GPU cores for tech. Redirect some workers to GPU deposits.
prompt_variants: We need GPU cores. Send some workers to tech ruins.
Redirect workers to gather GPU cores.
Priority shift: food is fine, get GPU cores now.
Need tech resources — assign gatherers to GPU deposits.

scenarios.name: Early production building
scenarios.description: Build first CatTree to start army production.
prompt_variants: Build a CatTree near our base.
Start army production — we need a barracks.
Get a CatTree up ASAP.
Build production: CatTree near TheBox.

scenarios.name: Supply building
scenarios.description: Supply capped, need to build LitterBox.
prompt_variants: We're supply capped. Build a LitterBox.
Need more supply — can't train units.
Supply blocked. Get a LitterBox built.
Population cap reached, expand supply.

scenarios.name: Tech building
scenarios.description: Build ServerRack for AI upgrades and advanced capabilities.
prompt_variants: Build a ServerRack for tech upgrades.
Time to tech up — get a ServerRack.
We need AI capabilities. Build tech building.
Invest in ServerRack for advanced units.

scenarios.name: Defensive buildings
scenarios.description: Build CatFlap walls or LaserPointer turrets for base defense.
prompt_variants: Fortify the base — build some walls and turrets.
Set up defenses. CatFlap and LaserPointer.
Enemy might attack soon. Build defensive structures.
Secure the perimeter with defensive buildings.

scenarios.name: Build balanced army
scenarios.description: Train a mix of tanks and ranged from production buildings.
prompt_variants: Build a balanced army — tanks and ranged.
Train some Chonks and Hissers.
We need frontline and damage. Mix it up.
Produce an army that can fight well.

scenarios.name: Counter enemy composition
scenarios.description: Enemy has specific composition, train counter units.
prompt_variants: They're going heavy tanks. What should we train?
Enemy is all Chonks. Counter them.
Need to counter their tank-heavy army.
They're massing Chonks. Build the right response.

scenarios.name: Emergency army rebuild
scenarios.description: Lost most of army, need to rapidly rebuild.
prompt_variants: Army destroyed. Rebuild fast!
Emergency — produce units from all buildings.
We lost everything. Spam units NOW.
Rapid rebuild — train from every production building.

scenarios.name: Use terrain cover
scenarios.description: Position ranged units in cover (Forest/Rock) for damage reduction.
prompt_variants: Move ranged units into the forest for cover.
Use terrain — get our Hissers behind cover.
Position army using terrain advantage.
Find cover for our units before engaging.

scenarios.name: Elevation advantage
scenarios.description: Use high ground for +15% damage per elevation level.
prompt_variants: Take the high ground before engaging.
Move to elevated terrain for damage bonus.
Use elevation advantage — get uphill.
Position on the ramp for combat advantage.

scenarios.name: Navigate around obstacles
scenarios.description: Route army around impassable terrain or through favorable terrain.
prompt_variants: Route the army around the water.
Find a path to the enemy avoiding rough terrain.
Navigate around obstacles to reach their base.
Check if we can reach the enemy position and find the best path.

scenarios.name: Complete combat micro
scenarios.description: Full combat script: classification + focus fire + formation + kiting + push.
prompt_variants: Write me a complete combat micro script.
Full tactical AI — handle all combat situations.
Combat controller: focus fire, kite, push, the works.
Write the best combat micro you can.
Complete combat management — adapt to the situation.

scenarios.name: Economy with defensive posture
scenarios.description: Manage economy while maintaining defensive readiness.
prompt_variants: Keep gathering but be ready to defend.
Economy mode with defense — workers gather, army guards.
Balance economy and defense posture.
Gather resources but keep combat units on guard.

scenarios.name: Scout then commit
scenarios.description: Scout enemy position, then decide whether to attack or defend.
prompt_variants: Scout the enemy then decide if we attack.
Send a Nuisance to scout, then plan our move.
Recon first, then commit based on what we find.
We need intel. Scout then attack if favorable.

scenarios.name: Production + aggressive push
scenarios.description: Keep producing units while pushing with existing army.
prompt_variants: Attack with what we have and keep training more units.
Push the enemy while producing reinforcements.
Two-track: army attacks, buildings produce.
Keep the pressure on and train more troops.

scenarios.name: Push or defend decision
scenarios.description: Decide whether to push forward or defend based on game state.
prompt_variants: Should we attack or defend? Assess the situation.
Decide: push forward or turtle up.
Evaluate if it's time to attack or hold.
Strategic assessment — attack or wait?

scenarios.name: Building priority decision
scenarios.description: Decide what to build next based on current needs.
prompt_variants: What should we build next?
Prioritize our next building — what do we need most?
Assess our needs and decide what to construct.
Next build order step — what's the priority?

scenarios.name: Target priority in push
scenarios.description: Which enemy building to target first during a push.
prompt_variants: We're at their base. What do we destroy first?
Target priority: which building to attack?
We broke through. Hit production or HQ first?
In their base — what's the target priority?

scenarios.name: No combat units
scenarios.description: Player asks for combat but has only workers.
prompt_variants: Attack the enemy!
Send everything to fight.
Charge! Attack with all units.

scenarios.name: Refuse army splitting request
scenarios.description: Player asks to split army — script should push back politely.
prompt_variants: Split the army and harass their workers.
Send half the army to raid, keep half defending.
Detach a squad to go after their economy.
I want to harass — send a few units behind enemy lines.

scenarios.name: No resources available
scenarios.description: Player asks to build/train but has insufficient resources.
prompt_variants: Train more units!
Build a CatTree.
Expand — build more buildings.

scenarios.name: Simple one-liner tasks
scenarios.description: Tasks that should be very concise scripts.
prompt_variants: Stop all units.
How many units do I have?
Set rally point for my CatTree.
Hold position with all combat units.
